name: Mercury
description: Smallest planet, closest to the Sun.
funFacts: A year on Mercury is just 88 Earth days.
Despite being closest to the Sun, it is not the hottest planet.
Mercury has no atmosphere to retain heat.

name: Venus
description: Hottest planet due to thick atmosphere.
funFacts: Venus rotates backwards compared to most planets.
A day on Venus is longer than its year.
Surface pressure is 90 times that of Earth.

name: Earth
description: Our home planet, only known planet with life.
funFacts: 71% of Earth's surface is covered in water.
Earth is the densest planet in the solar system.
Earth's atmosphere protects us from harmful solar radiation.

name: Mars
description: The Red Planet, has the largest volcano.
funFacts: Olympus Mons on Mars is the tallest volcano in the solar system.
Mars has two small moons: Phobos and Deimos.
A Martian day is only 37 minutes longer than an Earth day.

name: Jupiter
description: Largest planet, famous for its Great Red Spot.
funFacts: Jupiter's Great Red Spot is a storm older than 350 years.
Jupiter has the shortest day of all planets at just 10 hours.
More than 1,300 Earths could fit inside Jupiter.

name: Saturn
description: Known for its stunning ring system.
funFacts: Saturn is the least dense planet and could float on water.
Its rings are made of ice and rock particles.
Saturn has 146 known moons, including Titan which has a thick atmosphere.

name: Uranus
description: Rotates on its side, ice giant.
funFacts: Uranus rotates at a 98 degree tilt, essentially on its side.
It is the coldest planetary atmosphere in the solar system.
Uranus has 13 known rings.

name: Neptune
description: Farthest planet, has the strongest winds.
funFacts: Winds on Neptune can reach 2,100 km/h, the fastest in the solar system.
Neptune was the first planet predicted mathematically before being observed.
One year on Neptune equals 165 Earth years.

name: Pluto
description: Reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006.
funFacts: Pluto was considered the 9th planet for 76 years.
Pluto's largest moon Charon is half the size of Pluto itself.
A day on Pluto lasts about 6.4 Earth days.

name: The Sun
description: The star at the center of our solar system.
funFacts: The Sun contains 99.86% of the mass of the entire solar system.
Light from the Sun takes about 8 minutes to reach Earth.
The Sun is about 4.6 billion years old and halfway through its life.
